2604.23666.title: An AI-Based Supervisory Measurement Integrity Validation Layer for Cyber-Resilient AC/DC Protection in Inverter-Based Microgrids
2604.23666.paper_summary: Line current differential relays (LCDRs) are measurement-driven relays that rely on time-synchronized multi-phase current waveforms to infer internal faults in AC and DC power networks. In inverter-based microgrids, however, the increasing reliance on digitally communicated measurements exposes LCDRs to false-data injection attacks (FDIAs), in which adversaries manipulate remote measurement streams to create protection-triggering yet physically inconsistent current trajectories. This paper addresses this emerging measurement integrity problem by introducing a measurement integrity validation scheme that operates as a supervisory instrumentation layer for modern LCDRs. The proposed scheme interprets short windows of synchronized instantaneous current measurements recorded during relay operation and assesses their physical consistency to distinguish genuine fault-induced trajectories from cyber-manipulated measurement streams. A recurrent neural network is trained offline using only relay-available current measurements and exploits the temporal structure of differential current waveforms, which remains informative in inverter-dominated systems where current magnitude is no longer a reliable observable. The method requires no additional sensors, auxiliary protection elements, or prior knowledge of network topology, and is applicable to both AC and DC LCDRs without structural modification. The proposed measurement validation scheme is evaluated on an islanded inverter-based microgrid under a comprehensive set of fault and FDIA scenarios, demonstrating high detection accuracy while preserving relay dependability. Hardware-in-the-loop validation using an OPAL-RT real-time simulator confirms that the scheme satisfies protection timing constraints and can operate in real time under realistic operating conditions.
2604.23666.paper_summary_zh: 線電流差動繼電器（LCDRs）是基於測量的繼電器，依賴於時間同步的多相電流波形來推斷交流和直流電力網絡中的內部故障。<lb/>然而，在基於逆變器的微電網中，對數位通信測量的日益依賴使LCDRs暴露於虛假數據注入攻擊（FDIAs），在這種攻擊中，對手操縱遠程測量流，以創造觸發保護但在物理上不一致的電流軌跡。<lb/>本文針對這一新興的測量完整性問題，提出了一種測量完整性驗證方案，該方案作為現代LCDRs的監控儀器層運行。<lb/>所提出的方案解釋了在繼電器運行期間記錄的同步瞬時電流測量的短時間窗口，並評估其物理一致性，以區分真正的故障引起的軌跡與網絡操縱的測量流。<lb/>一個 recurrent neural network 在離線情況下僅使用繼電器可用的電流測量進行訓練，並利用差動電流波形的時間結構，這在電流幅度不再是可靠可觀測量的逆變器主導系統中仍然具有信息價值。<lb/>該方法不需要額外的傳感器、輔助保護元件或網絡拓撲的先驗知識，並且適用於交流和直流LCDRs，無需結構修改。<lb/>所提出的測量驗證方案在一個孤立的基於逆變器的微電網上進行評估，涵蓋了一系列故障和FDIA場景，顯示出高檢測準確率，同時保持繼電器的可靠性。<lb/>使用OPAL-RT實時模擬器進行的硬體在迴路驗證確認該方案滿足保護時間約束，並能在現實操作條件下實時運行。
2604.23666.author: Ahmad Mohammad Saber et.al.
2604.23666.authors: Ahmad Mohammad Saber, Ahmed Saber Refae, Davor Svetinovic, Hatem Zeineldin, Amr Youssef, Ehab F. El-Saadany, Deepa Kundur
